name: Quarterly Business Review
summary: Standard QBR / business-review arc for internal leadership reviews. Recap the period, show what the numbers say, extract insights, lay out the plan, surface risks, and request the asks the team needs to execute.

beats.name: Recap
beats.description: Briefly remind the audience what the team committed to last quarter and the high-level results against those commitments. Keeps the review grounded in prior context rather than starting from scratch.

beats.name: Performance
beats.description: Present the numbers — KPIs, OKRs, targets vs actuals. Use clear charts with the headline metric and direction-of-travel obvious at a glance. Call out both wins and misses honestly.

beats.name: Insights
beats.description: Translate the numbers into 'so what.' What does the data tell you about customers, the market, your operations, your bets? Insights should be opinions backed by evidence, not restatements of the metrics.

beats.name: Plan
beats.description: Lay out the next quarter's plan: top priorities, target outcomes, sequencing. Each priority should trace clearly back to an insight or commitment. Avoid laundry lists — focus on what changes because of what you just learned.

beats.name: Risks
beats.description: Name the risks to the plan honestly. Dependencies, assumptions, capacity gaps, market factors. For each risk, state the mitigation or the open question the audience can help resolve.

beats.name: Asks
beats.description: Specific, named requests of the audience. Decisions needed, resources required, escalations to unblock. End with clarity on what happens next and who owns it.
